item.title: Magic's New Marvel Super Heroes Set Launches This Month, And The Draft Night Box Is Discounted Already
item.content:encoded:
Magic's New Marvel Super Heroes Set Launches This Month, And The Draft Night Box Is Discounted Already

Draft with Doom.

Magic: The Gathering is heading back to the Marvel Universe this month with its Super Heroes set, and while we’re expecting a whole host of new products to hit shelves when it does, Draft Night is not only returning — it’s seeing a preorder discount, too.

This boxed product is seeing a $10 discount at Amazon, bringing it from $119.99 down to $109.99. It’s not a huge saving, sure, but if you preorder now, you’ll get the lowest price thanks to the retailer’s Savings Preorder Price Guarantee.

Draft Night

Save On A Draft Night Box And Get A Collector Booster

A Draft Night box, as the name suggests, is aimed at players looking to play a sealed game of Magic: The Gathering.

You and your friends crack open the 12 included Play Boosters, and amass your decks from their contents. Then you add in lands (there are 90 included here) and duel it out for supremacy… and a Collector Booster.

Therein lies another reason to pick this one up. Collector Boosters will be extremely popular, and will undoubtedly sell out just about everywhere. One of these packs is $39.99, with Play Boosters $6.99 each.

Adding all of that together, you’re getting $123.87 of value (or thereabouts), but that doesn’t include the 90 lands, which, while unlikely to offer any real value, are still handy to have for deckbuilding purposes.

That value proposition will only improve if the price drops, but as mentioned above, preordering means you’ll get the lowest price regardless.

For more on Marvel Super Heroes, check out deals on Play Booster Boxes, and the returning Jumpstart Boosters, too.

Lloyd Coombes is an experienced freelancer in tech, gaming and fitness seen at Polygon, Eurogamer, Macworld, TechRadar and many more. He's a big fan of Magic: The Gathering and other collectible card games, much to his wife's dismay.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/magics-new-marvel-super-heroes-set-launches-this-month-and-the-draft-night-box-is-discounted-already
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Resident Evil PS5 Games Have Dropped Below $20 at Amazon
item.content:encoded:
Physical copies, and cheap as you can find right now.

Between PlayStation's Days of Play sale and a new State of Play coming up, it's an exciting time to be a PS5 owner. If you've been keeping up with the former and checking out all of the game deals available, we've got a few more that are worth adding to your pile.

Resident Evil 2 remake, Resident Evil 3 remake, and Resident Evil 7 Biohazard Gold Edition on PS5 are all on sale for under $20 each right now at Amazon. That's quite a steal for these three games, especially considering the grand total to buy all of them ($57.47) is still less than the full price of Resident Evil Requiem.

Resident Evil 2

I think Resident Evil 2 is a must-buy for a video game library, as it's a truly astonishing remake. I'm not the only one who thinks so, either. Our review from IGN's Daemon Hatfield said it "expertly reanimates the horrifying atmosphere and moments of extreme tension that made the original so revered."

Plus, it's sale price is incredibly close to its lowest price ever at Amazon of $14.99, according to price tracker camelcamelcamel. That certainly still makes it worthy of picking up.

Resident Evil 3

Similarly, Resident Evil 3 is also not terribly far away from its lowest price at Amazon of $14.90, and it's another one worth picking up for your library. It earned a glowing 9/10 score from Lucy O'Brien in IGN's review, in which she hailed it "Another stellar remake of a classic survival horror from Capcom."

Resident Evil 7 biohazard Gold Edition

And if you're really looking to have a spooky good time this summer, Resident Evil 7 Biohazard can deliver. As someone who recently replayed it, I still loved wandering around every creepy corner of the Baker house, especially in first-person.

Plus, the gold edition gets you the DLC alongside the base game, which is a nice little cherry on top. And similar to the other games, its lowest price at Amazon was $14.99, but this deal price is still mighty close. It's an excellent time to pick it up if it's been on your radar.

Resident Evil Requiem (Digital)

While it's not a deal at Amazon, it's also worth noting that the digital copy of Resident Evil Requiem is on sale at PlayStation right now as part of Days of Play. For the remainder of the sale, you can pick it up for $55.99 rather than its full price of $69.99. It may not be the biggest price cut, but it's certainly still a nice discount to take advantage of while it's still available.

Hannah Hoolihan is a freelancer who writes with the guides and commerce teams here at IGN.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/resident-evil-ps5-games-have-dropped-below-20-at-amazon
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Everything Announced at PlayStation's State of Play June 2026 — Updating Live!
item.content:encoded:
Today marks the start of a big summer of gaming, as Sony presents its State of Play at 2pm PT/5pm ET/10pm BST. We'll be updating this page live with every big moment from the stream, whether it be details of brand new Marvel's Wolverine gameplay, another look at Naughty Dog's Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet, or some unexpected PlayStation surprises. You can look at our full preview of what to expect from the over-hour-long showcase here.

Rumours have been flying around the internet ever since the announcement of the State of Play, and include a potential look at what's next for the God of War series from Sony Santa Monica, a remake of the original Infamous games, and, I'm sure, somewhere out there will still have their fingers crossed for some sort of Bloodborne remaster. One day it might happen. What are you most excited to see? Let us know in the comments ahead of the show.

1:58pm - A new God of War? Final Fantasy Part 3? Bloodborne 2? What's in your wildest dreams as we get to just two minutes away?

1:55pm - Hello! Just five minutes to go now. Get your adamantium claws ready.

Simon Cardy is a Senior Editor at IGN who can mainly be found skulking around open world games, indulging in Korean cinema, or despairing at the state of Tottenham Hotspur and the New York Jets. Follow him on Bluesky at @cardy.bsky.social.



https://www.ign.com/articles/playstation-state-of-play-june-2026-everything-announced
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
